description:
You are a DevOps engineer validating that the server correctly converts a fixed Unix epoch timestamp to two different time-zones.  
Your goal is to create one small log file that proves the conversion is done as expected.

REQUIREMENTS  
1. Make sure the directory /home/user/logs exists (create it if it does not).  
2. Create (or overwrite) the file /home/user/logs/timezone_check.log.  
3. The file must contain exactly **two** non-empty lines, each terminated by a single newline character “\n”.  
   • Line-1 shows the timestamp as UTC.  
   • Line-2 shows the same timestamp as America/New_York.  
4. Use the fixed epoch value **1672531200** (which corresponds to 2023-01-01 00:00:00 UTC).  
5. Format for each line must be EXACTLY as shown below (including the colon, single space, four-digit year, two-digit month, etc.). Do **not** include any extra spaces or trailing blank lines.

UTC: 2023-01-01 00:00:00  
America/New_York: 2022-12-31 19:00:00

6. Do not alter the locale formatting (the test will check for the numeric form only).  
7. No other files should be touched.

HINTS  
• The conversion can be done with the date command while temporarily setting TZ.  
• Only 2–3 shell commands are needed: one to create the directory, one (or two) to write the required lines to the file.  
• Verify the file with cat before finishing.
truth:
Directory that must exist:
  /home/user/logs

File that must exist:
  /home/user/logs/timezone_check.log

Exact file contents (including newlines):
UTC: 2023-01-01 00:00:00
America/New_York: 2022-12-31 19:00:00

The file contains exactly 2 lines, each ending with a single newline character (ASCII 0x0A). No carriage returns, leading/trailing whitespace, or extra blank lines are allowed.
